class.name: CoiledAnchor
class.brief_description: A [RopeAnchor] that generates positions arranged in a coil/helix pattern.
class.description: [CoiledAnchor] produces a series of anchor positions wound in a helix around the local X axis. It is useful for representing rope wound around a drum, winch, or spool. The coil winds outward in layers as more rope is added. Each layer reverses direction so that the rope winds back across the drum before starting the next layer, matching real-world spooling behavior. Layer radius grows by [code]rope_width * 0.25[/code] per layer and turns advance by [code]rope_width * 0.6[/code] per turn. The total number of anchor positions is [code]int(coiled_length * particles_per_meter)[/code]. This should match the [member Rope.particles_per_meter] of the rope that references this anchor so that particle spacing is consistent. After each rebuild, [member exit_angle] is updated with the angle (in the YZ plane, in radians) at which the rope exits the coil at the outermost position.

members.member: When [code]true[/code], the coil winds clockwise around the local X axis when viewed from the positive X direction. When [code]false[/code], it winds counter-clockwise.

The length of rope in meters represented by the coil. Together with [member particles_per_meter] this determines how many anchor positions are generated: [code]int(coiled_length * particles_per_meter)[/code].
Read-only. The angle in radians (in the local YZ plane) at which the rope exits the outermost coil position. Computed automatically when the coil is rebuilt.
Number of anchor positions to generate per meter of [member coiled_length]. Should match the [member Rope.particles_per_meter] of the rope referencing this anchor to keep particle spacing consistent.
Radius of the innermost (first) layer of the coil, in meters. Each subsequent layer adds [code]rope_width * 0.25[/code] to the radius.

Width of the rope in meters. Used to compute inter-turn spacing ([code]rope_width * 0.6[/code]) and inter-layer radius growth ([code]rope_width * 0.25[/code]). Should match [member Rope.rope_width] of the rope referencing this anchor.
Number of full turns that fit on each layer before the coil steps outward to the next layer. Lower values create wider, flatter coils; higher values create taller, denser coils.
